answer:
**In Manusmṛti (also known as the Manusmṛti or Laws of Manu) Chapter 8, the rules on lending money at interest appear primarily in verses 140–155 (Manusmṛti 8.140–155), as part of the broader discussion of the first of the "eighteen titles of law": non-payment of debt (ṛṇābhidhāna).**[^1]

This section follows rules on debt recovery (8.47–60) and witness testimony (8.61–140), then covers:
- Permissible monthly interest rates: 1.25% (1/80th of principal, per Vasiṣṭha), or up to 2% without sinning.[^2]
- Varna-differentiated rates: 2% (for Brāhmaṇas), 3% (Kṣatriyas), 4% (Vaiśyas), 5% (Śūdras).[^3]
- Limits and prohibitions: No interest on usable pledges; no time-based forfeiture or sale of pledges; maximum simple interest of double principal (or fivefold for grain, etc.); bans on compound, periodic, stipulated, or labor-based interest; fines for usury (excess interest).[^4]

**A moneylender may take the interest prescribed by Vasiṣṭha for the increase of his capital, taking the eightieth part per month on each hundred. Or, remembering the Dharma of the good, he may take two percent; for by taking two percent, he does not become a sinner for the sake of wealth. Two, three, four, and five percent per month, he may take as interest, in due order of the *varṇas*.**[^5]

These rules emphasize ethical limits to prevent exploitation while allowing regulated lending.[^6]

[^1]: Manusmṛti, *Chapter 8*, [full chapter](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/).

[^2]: Manusmṛti 8.140–141, [passage](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/#p-85e12e).

[^3]: Manusmṛti 8.142, [passage](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/#p-b774d8).

[^4]: Manusmṛti 8.143–155, [passage](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/#p-73fef8).

[^5]: Manusmṛti 8.140–142, [passage](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/#p-85e12e).

[^6]: Manusmṛti 8.152, [passage](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/#p-6f8e7d).
